what_it_does: This file is a channel-memory attachment ledger entry that records an image attachment (att-c2782399) in the design-assets channel and points it to a target image URI.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, no body content
standouts: Target URI contains an unescaped space and parentheses: 'image (56)'
No filename extension or MIME type is recorded
Entry is extremely minimal beyond identity and pointer
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with a stable att-* id
Uses a custom image:// URI scheme for asset targeting
Channel-memory ledger pattern: id, channel, type, target, added
concerns: Target path contains literal spaces and parentheses which may break naive URI parsing or filesystem mapping
No checksum, size, or version metadata for the referenced image
attachment_type 'images' is plural and generic compared to channel name 'design-assets'
notes: A bare-bones design-assets image attachment record that links an id to an image URI with a space/parenthesis in its name.
questions: What does '(56)' signify in the target path?
Is 'image (56)' a filename, a display label, or a generated thumbnail?
What system consumes the image:// URI?
Who added this attachment and from what source?
purpose_vs_actual: The file's purpose is to record an image attachment, and that is exactly what it does; it matches.
intention_signals: It appears to be registering a specific icon/image asset in the design-assets channel so it can be referenced by attachment id elsewhere.
novelty: The target URI embeds a space and parentheses literally, which is uncommon for asset pointers and may encode a user-visible label rather than a normalized path.
contribution: It contributes a concrete, timestamped mapping between an attachment identifier and a design-assets image target, preserving when the attachment was added.
summary_for_embedding: A channel-memory attachment record for the design-assets channel that maps attachment id att-c2782399 to the image target image://design-assets/icons/image (56) with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To register and persist a reference to a specific design-assets image so it can be retrieved or cited by its attachment id.
